dc-record.title: The Clifton Family Visit Russian River: A guide to the Vacation Wonderland
Magazine
dc-record.description: A twenty page third annual guide to the resorts and businesses of the Russian River published by the Guerneville Times. The narrative is of a fictional Clinton family from Iowa who visits various River recreational spots. The Guide is filled with sepia toned photos illustrating many of the resorts and businesses on the River including Angelo's, Mirabel Park Resort, Forest Hills Markt, Reno's Drive In, Lookout Cottages, Palm Beach Club, Britts,Rio Nido Resourt, DeCar's Shell Servcie, Roaland Beach, De Smit's Resort, Redwood Garden Chapel, the Surrey Inn, Scanlon's Gift Shop, Burchanan's and River Rendezvous, Gori's Tavern, Guerneville Bank, Meadows Court, Bucks Ranch, Negri's, the Mayor's Inn (Duncans Mills) Guerneville Elementary School, the Burl Shop at Armstrong Woods, Odd Fellows Recreation Club, Guernewood Park, Spooner's Guerewood Village Resort, Northwood Golf Course, Guernewood Food Market, Russian River Inn, Sportsman's Club, Rio Theater, Karen's Bakery, Bartlett's, Libonati's Beach Club and Restaurant, Torr Realty, Reagan's Department Store, Korbel & Santa Nella Wineries, Kohler's Hardware, Noonan's Market, Highland Dell Hotel Resort, There is also some coverage of Forestville, Sebastopol, and Santa Rosa. Back cover features Markarinans River Club.
dc-record.subject: Magazine
Vacation Wonderland Magazine

Newspaper
dc-record.description: West Sonoma County Paper, volume 7, number 38 Feb 26 - March 5, 1986 A special edition of "The Paper" that chronicles the data and effects of the flood of 1986 which recorded 49' 1" at Guerneville Bridge. Note: three copies of this newpaper edition are located in the 1986 Newspaper Box for the West Sonoma County Paper. "

dc-record.title: RUSSIAN RIVER A Guide to the Vacation Wonderland
Magazine
dc-record.description: A eighteen page fourth annual guide to the resorts and businesses of the Russian River published by the Guerneville Times.The Guide is filled with sepia toned photos illustrating many of the resorts and businesses on the River arranged by town: Forestville (Trading Post, Ideal Hardware, Holiday House, Lane Furniture, Don's Cocktail Lounge, Carlson's Department Store, Frediani's Inn, Forestville Market, Skippy's Hacienda Inn, Sprouse Reitx, and Modern Appliance & Furniture. Rio Nido show photos of Lookout Cottages, Palm Beach Club and Lee Mino Appliances & Furniture. & Jimmy Britts. Guerneville features a phto of Princess Pale Moon from the "Pageant of Fire Mountain", Pickard Shows, De Smit's Resourt, Buchanan's & River Rendezvouis, Gori's Tavern, Surrey Inn, Omar Villa Resort, Pee Week Golf, Redwood Lodge Gift Shop, Meadows Court. The centerfold is a map showing the fishing holes on the River. Guernewood Park shows the Guernewood Park Hotel-Resort, Spooner's Guernewoo Village Resort, Northwood Golf Course, and Russian River Inn. The section on Monte Rio features photos of the Highland Dell Hotel, Fern's Grocery, Noonan's Market, Regan's Department Store, Redwood Grove Resort, Pink Elephant, Rio Theater, Bartletts, Torr Readlty, and Bob's Market. An article on the "modern Monte Rio firehouse shows the former firestation which was the Monte Rio Railway Station (on site of current parking lot). A section give location and times for church services in the area. There is also an article "Redwood Empire Apple Items Produced on Big Scale by O.A. Hallberg & Sons of Graton. Inside Back cover features Buck's Ranch and the back cover features indoor and outdoor photos of Markarinans River Club.
dc-record.subject: Magazine
Vacation Wonderland Magazine

dc-record.title: RUSSIAN RIVER A Guide to the Vacation Wonderland
Magazine
dc-record.description: A eighteen page fifth annual guide to the resorts and businesses of the Russian River published by the Guerneville Times. An introductory article "The Church Built from one Russian River Redwood Tree Cut and Sawed at Guerneville" tell the story of the redwood tree that provided the wood for First Baptist Church in Santa Rosa. Thee is also an article on "Logging" with photos of the Stumptown Daze Parade with tractors (Riverview Logging) and trucks with logs. A photos essay shows some entries in the Stumptown Daze Parade. . Among the many business illustrated by photos: Carlaw's Nursery, Riviera Pizza, Ideal Hardware, Fay's Cafe, Jimmy Brit's, Pennant Cottages and Pennant Club, Frediani's Inn, Guernewood Park Realty, Guerneville Elementary School, Guernevill Bank of Sonoma, De Smit's, River Bakery, Gor Tavern, Redwood Lawn Resort, King's is featured in an article "Fishing is Great!". The quonset hut entrance to Amstrong Redwood State Park is shown as is the "Mural in Redwwod Graden Chapel" Special Summer Events are listed: Dolphin Water Carnival, Stumptionw Daze, Armstong Grove Concerts, Folk Dancing and Amateur Hour, Pageant of Fire Mountain, Steehed Derby. The section on Monte Rio shows a overhead shot of the train coming into Monte Rio from Occidental and a modern (1950's) view in the opposite direction. The is also a photo of Monte Rio's beaches before (pedestrian bridge) and now (auto bridge). A photo of the "Holiday Inn Cafe", Detes', the Mayor's Inn, Inside Back cover features Buck's Ranch and the back cover features indoor and outdoor photos of Markarinans River Club.
dc-record.subject: Magazine
Vacation Wonderland Magazine

dc-record.title: The Russian Rivers
Book
dc-record.description: Historical Fiction: "Along the harsh north California coast the otter swarm, enough to fill the Russians' trading ships with valuable pets. There they raised an outpost. Into this wild land, stripped of his rank for marrying an Indian girl, Lieutenant Anton Rostov is cast. He is a man without a country. He can hunt, trap and conquer. He discovers a power greater than his allegiance to a faraway Czar...the lure of a new world and the sweeping current of the Russian River.

Book
dc-record.description: The subtitle says it all. a collection of stories and memories from one of the most entertaining characters.. a minister, musician and amateur historian, Bob Jones. Chapters feature: The Day the Pig Ate Jimmy Neeley The Pageant of Fire Mountain Ruth Mowdry, the "River Lady" Lord, Plant my Feet on Higher Ground (Oscar Birkhofer, Gordon & Jonathan Herr, Margaret Laughlin, Dick Norberg) Holding Hands with Naomi Geiger The Cugley and the Ragles He Left his Heart in Rio Nido (Larry Miinches) Another Birthday for Anne Vorhes Death Row Chaplin (Reverend Byron Eshelman) Father George (Monaghan) and the Greatest Golf Shot Ever Bill Byrd for President Chuckles, Belly Laughs and Our Final Resting Place Little Johey Koche Why Two Churches Back in the Day The Gospel According to Inza Lambert A Mayor? For Guerneville? (Bob Schneider) Jazz and Skin Along the River The Cheapest Funeral He Ever Saw And God Said, "Oscar Old Buddy..." (Oscar Birkofer) Jerry the Gypsy Win One for the Skipper Death of a Language (Mabel McKay) (Pomo language) Dogs of August Festival for Clive (Clive Hawthorne) Floods of '95 Eye to Ey with Dino the Dinosaur (Pee Wee Golf) Gay Ice In Memory of Rich (Don't Call Me Richard) Wilson (Bob St. Clair) Uncle Leaky Catches Flies (Babe Fulkerson) Jessie King Mr. Fort Ross (John MKenzie) Joe Goggin: War Hero, Song Writer, Friend Elva Cuthill, One of a Kind Zona's Buick Gets A Wax Job (Zona Schubert) Doc Ellison and Father Philaret Sounds of a Last Farewell (Oscar and Betty Mann) Marker Markarian The Ring Bearer Was a German Shepard (Greg King) The Stewart Sisters of Hacienda A Guy Named Tom (Tom Walsh) Goodbye to Marigold (Marigold Hill) Dr. Charles Schaap Benny the Bebop Drummer Turns Eighty (Benny Barth) Red's Sendoff (Red Gambetta) James Stadig's Third Hole-in-One The First Parson Jones (William Ladd Jones) Two Parades in One Day (John Schubert, Bob Dewitt, Bob Evangelist, River Ranger, Sister Glitz, Bob Burke) First Day of Spring (Riverlane Resort)
